studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1: Culturally adapted digital cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) (Somryst), including access to the digital program, usual care by primary care provider, and a sleep hygiene brochure. Arm 2: Minimally Enhanced Usual Care (mEUC), including usual care by primary care provider, pharmacotherapy if applicable, and a sleep hygiene brochure.
studies.collected_data: Primary outcomes: Change in Mean Insomnia Severity Index Score (ISI) from baseline to post-intervention (9 weeks) and baseline to 6 months post-intervention. Secondary outcomes: Change in Mean Quality of Life Score (Short-Form 12 [SF-12], MCS), Change in Mean Daytime Sleepiness (Epworth Sleepiness Scale [ESS]), Change in Mean Satisfaction With Care (Patient Assessment of Chronic Illness Care [PACIC]). Other outcomes: Change in Mean Sleep Quality (Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index [PSQI]), Change in Mean Self-reported Wake After Sleep Onset (14-day sleep diary), Change in Mean Actigraphy Assessed Wake After Sleep Onset (14-day wrist-actigraphy), Change in Mean Self-reported Sleep Onset Latency (14-day sleep diary).
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Primary outcome: ISI score reduction at 9 weeks (CBT-I: -6.7, 95% CI [-15.4, 2.0]; mEUC: -3.9, 95% CI [-12.5, 4.7]) and at 6 months (CBT-I: -5.7, 95% CI [-17.1, 5.8]; mEUC: -1.6, 95% CI [-13.0, 9.9]). Secondary outcomes: SF-12 MCS score improvement at 9 weeks (CBT-I: 10.8, 95% CI [5.9, 15.7]; mEUC: 0.8, 95% CI [-4.0, 5.6]). ESS score change at 9 weeks (CBT-I: -2.3, 95% CI [-6.5, 1.9]; mEUC: 0.5, 95% CI [-3.6, 4.6]). PACIC score change at 9 weeks (CBT-I: 17.4, 95% CI [-20.5, 55.4]; mEUC: -8.2, 95% CI [-41.9, 25.6]). Other outcomes: PSQI score change at 9 weeks (CBT-I: -1.0, 95% CI [-2.6, 0.6]; mEUC: -0.7, 95% CI [-2.2, 0.8]). Actigraphy-assessed wake after sleep onset at 9 weeks (CBT-I: -2.5, 95% CI [-38.5, 33.6]; mEUC: -4.8, 95% CI [-10.2, 0.6]). Self-reported sleep onset latency at 9 weeks (CBT-I: -53.3, 95% CI [-128.6, 21.9]; mEUC: -16.9, 95% CI [-84.7, 51.0]).

studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group received the prescription digital therapeutic (PDT) for mobile-delivered CBT-I (Somryst); control group did not receive the PDT.
studies.collected_data: Insomnia Severity Index score, healthcare utilisation, health utility scores, clinical outcomes, sleep outcomes (measured with sleep diaries), patient-reported outcomes (depressive symptoms, daytime sleepiness, health status, stress, anxiety), engagement with the PDT.

studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: SHUTi: digital CBT-I intervention; HealthWatch: attention-matched control internet program
studies.collected_data: Online sleep diaries measuring sleep-onset latency (SOL), wake after sleep onset (WASO), sleep efficiency (SE), number of awakenings, sleep quality, and total sleep time (TST).
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Participants in the SHUTi condition had greater improvements at posttest compared with control for SOL, WASO, SE, number of awakenings, and sleep quality. These improvements were sustained at every follow-up (p < .02 for all outcomes except TST, in which statistically significant increases were observed only at 12- and 18-months).

studies.collected_data: Event type, patient demographics, outcomes, product details.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Two adverse event reports were identified: one involving Somryst for insomnia prescribed to a patient with a seizure disorder, resulting in disability, and another involving EndeavorRx, reporting perceived ineffectiveness without clinical harm. No adverse events were identified for the remaining 11 PDTs.

studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Single-arm study where participants accessed the FDA-cleared prescription digital therapeutic (Somryst®) delivering cognitive-behavioral therapy for insomnia.
studies.collected_data: Insomnia Severity Index (ISI), 8-item Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-8), Generalized Anxiety Disorder-7 scale (GAD-7), daily sleep diaries.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: For participants who completed all 6 treatment cores (48.4%), ISI scores decreased from 18.8 to 9.9 (P <.001). Scores remained lower than baseline at immediate post-intervention (11.0), 6-month (11.6), and 1-year follow-ups (12.2) (P <.001). PHQ-8 and GAD-7 scores also showed significant decreases at all measured timepoints from baseline (P <.001). 58% of participants were considered adherent, and 46.8% completed all 6 cores.

studies.collected_data: Insomnia Severity Index (ISI) scores, healthcare resource utilization (HCRU) data including emergency department visits, hospitalizations, hospital outpatient visits, ambulatory surgical center visits, office visits, prescription data, and total costs.
studies.key_outcomes_findings: Mean ISI score declined by 37.2% from baseline (19.1 vs 12.0) after 9 weeks; 58.8% of patients achieved ISI responder status (ISI score improved by =>7; NNT: 1.7); 26.6% achieved insomnia remission (ISI score <8; NNT for remission: 3.8). Two-year follow-up showed reductions in emergency department visits (-53%; IRR: 0.47; 95% CI 0.27, 0.82; P=0.008), hospitalizations (-21%; IRR: 0.79; 95% CI 0.46, 1.35; P=0.389), and hospital outpatient visits (-13%; IRR: 0.87; 95% CI 0.66, 1.14; P=0.315). Slight increases were observed for ambulatory surgical center visits (2%; IRR: 1.02; 95% CI 0.73, 1.44; P=0.903) and office visits (2%; IRR: 1.02; 95% CI 0.92, 1.14; P=0.672). The number of patients treated with sleep aid medications dropped 18.5% (52.4% pre-index vs 42.7% post-index). Average number of prescriptions decreased from 3.98 pre-index to 3.73 post-index (P=0.552). Total two-year cost reduction post-index vs pre-index was $510,678, or -$2059 per patient.

audit.study_title: Digital Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy for Insomnia in Spanish Speaking Latinas/os (Dormir Mejor Study)
Sleep-specific outcomes attributable to digitally delivered cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia in adults with insomnia and depressive symptoms.
Postmarket Safety Surveillance of FDA-Cleared Prescription Digital Therapeutics Using the Manufacturer and User Facility Device Experience (MAUDE) Database: A Pharmacovigilance Study.
Effect of a prescription digital therapeutic for chronic insomnia on post-treatment insomnia severity, depression, and anxiety symptoms: results from the real-world DREAM study.
Real-World Evaluation of Clinical Response and Long-Term Healthcare Resource Utilization Patterns Following Treatment with a Digital Therapeutic for Chronic Insomnia.
